data.content:
### Basic usage of ResponsiveContainer for a responsive chart Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/ResponsiveContainer.mdx This JSX snippet demonstrates how to wrap a Recharts chart component, such as `LineChart`, within a `ResponsiveContainer` to make it adapt to its parent's dimensions. It sets the width to fill 100% of the parent and a fixed height of 400px, allowing the child chart to automatically receive the calculated dimensions after client-side rendering. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### FunnelChart with ResponsiveContainer Parent Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/FunnelChart.mdx FunnelChart should be wrapped within a ResponsiveContainer component to enable responsive behavior and automatic sizing based on parent container dimensions. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### ResponsiveContainer with initialDimension for Server-Side Rendering (SSR) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/ResponsiveContainer.mdx This JSX example illustrates using `ResponsiveContainer` with the `initialDimension` prop to provide fallback dimensions for server-side rendering (SSR) environments. This ensures the chart renders with specified dimensions (e.g., 520x400) before client-side hydration, preventing content layout shifts while waiting for browser API calculations to determine actual dimensions. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Responsive Charts with Recharts ResponsiveContainer in React Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt This React component illustrates the use of Recharts' ResponsiveContainer to create charts that automatically adapt to their parent container's size. It shows examples of percentage-based sizing, aspect ratio constraints, and how to use the debounce and onResize props for performance optimization and callback functionality. The ResponsiveContainer wraps LineChart instances, ensuring fluid and dynamic chart layouts. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { ResponsiveContainer, LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Page A', uv: 4000 }, { name: 'Page B', uv: 3000 }, { name: 'Page C', uv: 2000 }, { name: 'Page D', uv: 2780 }, { name: 'Page E', uv: 1890 }, ]; function ResponsiveExample() { return (
{/* Percentage-based sizing */} {/* Aspect ratio constraint */} {/* With resize callback and debounce */} console.log(`Chart resized to ${width}x${height}`)} >
); } export default ResponsiveExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### PieChart Component Hierarchy Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx Documents the valid parent and child component relationships for PieChart in Recharts. PieChart must be wrapped in ResponsiveContainer and supports multiple child components for customization. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### ComposedChart Component Structure Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/ComposedChart.mdx The ComposedChart component is a container for composed chart visualizations that can display multiple data series with different chart types simultaneously. It requires a ResponsiveContainer parent and supports multiple child components for customization. ```APIDOC ## ComposedChart Component ### Description A flexible charting component that combines multiple chart types (Area, Line, Bar) in a single visualization. Used for displaying complex data with multiple dimensions and series. ### Component Hierarchy #### Parent Component - **ResponsiveContainer** - Required wrapper component that provides responsive sizing #### Child Components (Supported) - **XAxis** - Horizontal axis component - **YAxis** - Vertical axis component - **CartesianGrid** - Grid lines for better readability - **Tooltip** - Interactive data point information display - **Legend** - Chart legend for data series identification - **Area** - Area chart data series - **Line** - Line chart data series - **Bar** - Bar chart data series - **ReferenceArea** - Highlighted area reference zone - **ReferenceLine** - Horizontal or vertical reference line - **ReferenceDot** - Reference point marker - **Brush** - Data range selector component - **Customized** - Custom shape components - **Valid SVG Elements** - Additional SVG elements for custom rendering ### Properties Refer to ArgTypes documentation for complete property listing. Note: Properties in the 'Other' and 'Internal' groups are not recommended for use. ### Basic Usage Example ```jsx import { ComposedChart, Area, Line, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Jan', uv: 4000, pv: 2400, amt: 2400 }, { name: 'Feb', uv: 3000, pv: 1398, amt: 2210 } ]; ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Brush Component - Interactive Data Filtering and Zooming Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Implements an interactive brush selector to filter and zoom through large datasets in a line chart. The Brush component displays a preview of data with adjustable range handles (startIndex and endIndex). It accepts an onChange callback to track range modifications and works with ResponsiveContainer for responsive layouts. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { LineChart, Line, Brush, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const generateData = () => { const data = []; for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) { data.push({ name: `Point ${i}`, value: Math.random() * 1000 + 100, }); } return data; }; const data = generateData(); function BrushExample() { return ( {/* Brush for data filtering */} console.log('Brush range:', range)} > ); } export default BrushExample; ```
